channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: U.S. and Iran signal willingness for further negotiations despite renewed military escalation in West Asia
item.description: The live updates focus on the rapidly evolving conflict between the United States and Iran following a fresh round of military strikes that have raised concerns about a wider regional war. U.S. Presid...

item.title: SUV Hit-and-Run Leaves One Dead, Another Injured in Mandaluyong
item.description: A woman died and a motorcycle rider was injured after being hit by a sport utility vehicle (SUV) with the House of Representatives logo in Mandaluyong. The incident occurred around 4 a.m. on Ortigas A...

item.title: Canada Faces Gang Violence and Political Tensions
item.description: The article highlights key stories impacting Canada, including the influence of Lawrence Bishnoi, an Indian gangster linked to violent crimes in the country, and Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's financ...

item.title: AJK PM Condemns Misuse of JAAC Platform, Calls for Dialogue
item.description: Azad Jammu and Kashmir Prime Minister Faisal Mumtaz Rathore endorsed a senior Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC) leader's criticism of individuals misusing the organization's platform. In a video sha...

item.title: America's Smallest Bank Faces Collapse Amid Financial Struggles
item.description: The collapse of America's smallest bank, which operated in a rural community, highlights growing challenges in the financial sector. The bank, which had only a handful of customers and a modest balanc...

item.title: Simba SC Prepares for Kagame Cup with Pre-Season Training
item.description: Tanzanian football giants Simba SC are set to begin their pre-season training camp on July 18 in preparation for the CECAFA Kagame Cup, a regional tournament hosted in Rwanda from July 24 to August 8....

item.title: Tragic fire on Lake Victoria boat kills four, dozens rescued
item.description: A tragic fire aboard a passenger boat on Lake Victoria has claimed four lives, including a child, while dozens of passengers were rescued after jumping into the water. The incident occurred on July 9 ...

item.title: Keyamo and Obi clash over airport parking fine and political accusations
item.description: Minister of Aviation, Festus Keyamo, has accused presidential candidate Peter Obi of refusing to pay a fine after his vehicle was clamped at the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport. Keyamo released C...

item.title: Imogen Mashazi faces legal action over corruption allegations
item.description: The case of former Ekurhuleni city manager Imogen Mashazi has ignited widespread public discourse, with social media platforms witnessing intense reactions to the legal actions taken by law enforcemen...

item.title: The Physical Impact of Watching a Soccer Final on Fans, According to Science
item.description: A study reveals how intense emotions during a soccer final affect fans' physiology. Researchers tracked stress levels, heart rates, and cortisol spikes in spectators during a high-stakes match. Stadiu...

item.title: Everyday items endangering Australian wildlife: How to prevent entanglements
item.description: Thousands of native Australian animals are dying or suffering injuries from everyday items like fruit nets, fishing line, and fences. A study reveals over 3,500 entanglement cases annually in New Sout...

item.title: NKTI Urges Caution Against Medical Scams
item.description: The National Kidney and Transplant Institute (NKTI) has issued a public warning against individuals impersonating the institution to solicit payments for medical bills. The advisory highlights that sc...

item.title: Oyo Principal's Husband Shares Relief After Abduction
item.description: Professor Wale Alamu, husband of Oyo State principal Rachael Alamu, spoke about the emotional relief after his wife and others were released after eight weeks of captivity. He called on the government...

item.title: US sanctions on Russia energy buyers could impact India's trade relations
item.description: The article discusses the potential implications of US legislation targeting countries purchasing Russian energy, including India. Four US Senators, including Lindsey Graham and Richard Blumenthal, ha...

item.title: KMTC Student Found Dead in Nairobi Hostel
item.description: Anne Odhiambo Adhiambo, a Higher Diploma in Clinical Medicine student at Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC), was found dead in her hostel room at the Nairobi Campus. The incident occurred on July 7...

item.title: Strategies to Avoid Sunbed Conflicts in European Resorts
item.description: The article explores the growing issue of sunbed allocation disputes in European resorts and the innovative measures being implemented to resolve them. British holidaymakers, known for their competiti...

item.title: US and Iran Resume Military Tensions Amid Ongoing Negotiations
item.description: The United States has agreed to further negotiations with Iran despite declaring the ceasefire between the two nations as over. President Donald Trump reiterated that the truce is definitively ended, ...

item.title: 105-Year-Old Woman Trapped in Stairwell During Care Home Fire
item.description: A 105-year-old woman was trapped in a stairwell during a fire at a care home, prompting her family to demand answers about the incident. The event, reported on July 10, 2026, highlights concerns about...

item.title: Historical Events and Records from July 10th Across the World
item.description: The article highlights significant historical events occurring on July 10 across different centuries. Key points include extreme weather records such as Death Valley's 57°C in 1913, South Africa's 50°...

item.title: India Reviews Meta's Response to WhatsApp Usernames Feature; Rollout Delayed
item.description: The Indian government is reviewing Meta's response to concerns about WhatsApp's proposed username feature, which allows users to create unique identifiers without revealing phone numbers. The Ministry...
